steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states that the sculpture is exposed to rain, snow, heat, and humidity — conditions that would normally damage an artwork. The second sentence reveals it remains in good condition. The blank must signal that what follows is surprising or contrary to what the harsh conditions would suggest.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — That said,: concedes the preceding point (the harsh conditions) and pivots to something that qualifies or contradicts it (the sculpture's durability). This is exactly the concessive contrast the sentence needs. ✓
  • Choice B — In addition,: adds more information in the same direction as what came before. It would suggest the good condition is yet another harsh detail — which is the wrong direction entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — On the contrary,: flatly negates the previous statement. It would imply the sculpture is not actually exposed to those conditions — but the passage says it is. ✗
  • Choice D — As a result,: signals that the good condition is caused by the harsh conditions. That reverses the logic: the harsh conditions are precisely what would be expected to degrade, not preserve, the sculpture. ✗
A is correct.
